title: Paul Thomas Anderson Got His Oscars Due After a Best Picture Battle for the Ages
text: The Ringer analyzed Paul Thomas Anderson's Oscar success following his Golden Globe Best Director victory. The article contextualizes Anderson's achievement within his longer career trajectory, noting his three prior Oscar nominations for Best Director without wins ('There Will Be Blood,' 'Phantom Thread,' and 'Licorice Pizza'). The Ringer's perspective highlights how 'One Battle After Another' represented a breakthrough moment for Anderson in terms of major awards recognition. The film's success at the Golden Globes foreshadowed its later accomplishments at subsequent awards ceremonies. The Ringer's analysis serves as post-awards reflection on what the victories meant for Anderson's legacy and the film's place in contemporary cinema.
